# unknown

This file will become your README and also the index of your
documentation.

## Developer Guide

If you are new to using `nbdev` here are some useful pointers to get you
started.

### Install unknown in Development mode

``` sh
# make sure unknown package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to unknown
$ nbdev_prepare
```

## Usage

### Installation

Install latest from the GitHub
[repository](https://github.com/frangs/unknown):

``` sh
$ pip install git+https://github.com/frangs/unknown.git
```

or from [conda](https://anaconda.org/frangs/unknown)

``` sh
$ conda install -c frangs unknown
```

or from [pypi](https://pypi.org/project/unknown/)

``` sh
$ pip install unknown
```

### Documentation

Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub
[repository](https://github.com/frangs/unknown)’s
[pages](https://frangs.github.io/unknown/). Additionally you can find
package manager specific guidelines on
[conda](https://anaconda.org/frangs/unknown) and
[pypi](https://pypi.org/project/unknown/) respectively.
